prompt: Design it twice before we build anything. I want to add a new `/benchmark-triage` skill to this repo that reads a skill benchmark iteration directory and tells me what to change next. It needs to fit the patterns already used by `skill-creator`, `create-plan`, and `implement-plan`. Don't implement it yet — I want competing architectures first, and I care about reusability vs keeping the workflow lightweight.
assumptions: The new skill should run as a forked, self-contained workflow rather than an interactive one.
The skill should accept either an iteration directory or benchmark.json and may inspect nearby grading and eval artifacts.
The skill should produce actionable next-change recommendations, not only descriptive benchmark notes.
The desired architecture should align with create-plan and implement-plan by using explicit phases/gates and a strict final report contract.
No repository source files should be modified during this benchmark run.
